title: गायत्री मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गायत्री मन्त्रः ॥

॥ विनियोगः ॥
ॐ अस्य श्री गायत्री मन्त्रस्य विश्वामित्र ऋषिः।
गायत्री छन्दः। सविता देवता।
ॐ भूः बीजम्। ॐ भुवः शक्तिः। ॐ स्वः कीलकम्।
श्री सवितुः प्रीत्यर्थे गायत्री मन्त्रजपे विनियोगः॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
मुक्ताविद्रुमहेमनीलधवलच्छायैर्मुखैस्त्रीक्षणैः
युक्तामिन्दुनिबद्धरत्नमकुटां तत्त्वार्थवर्णात्मिकाम्।
गायत्रीं वरदाभयाङ्कुशकशाशूलं कपालं गदां
शंखं चक्रमथारविन्दयुगलं हस्तैर्वहन्तीं भजे॥

॥ मन्त्रः ॥
ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः
तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं
भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि
धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्॥

॥ अर्थः ॥
ॐ — परमात्मा का नाम
भूः — प्राण स्वरूप
भुवः — दुःख नाशक
स्वः — सुख स्वरूप
तत् — वह (परमात्मा)
सवितुः — सृष्टिकर्ता सविता (सूर्य) देवता का
वरेण्यं — वरण करने योग्य, श्रेष्ठ
भर्गः — पापनाशक तेज
देवस्य — दिव्य, देव का
धीमहि — हम ध्यान करें
धियः — बुद्धि को
यः — जो
नः — हमारी
प्रचोदयात् — शुभ मार्ग पर प्रेरित करें

॥ गायत्री शीर्ष ॥
ॐ आपो ज्योतीरसोऽमृतं ब्रह्म भूर्भुवः स्वरोम्॥

॥ इति श्री गायत्री मन्त्रः सम्पूर्णः ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gayatri Mantrah ||

|| Viniyogah ||
Om Asya Shri Gayatri Mantrasya Vishwamitra Rishiph.
Gayatri Chhandah. Savita Devata.
Om Bhuh Bijam. Om Bhuvah Shaktih. Om Svah Kilakam.
Shri Savituh Prityarthe Gayatri Mantra-Jape Viniyogah.

|| Dhyanam ||
Mukta-vidruma-hema-nila-dhavala-chchhayair-mukhaih-trikshanaiah
Yuktam-indu-nibaddha-ratna-makutam Tattvartha-varnatmikam.
Gayatrim Varada-bhaya-ankusha-kasha-shulam Kapalam Gadam
Shankham Chakram-atha-aravinda-yugalam Hastair-vahantim Bhaje.

|| Mantrah ||
Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah
Tat Savitur Varenyam
Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi
Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat.

|| Arthah ||
Om — Paramatma ka Nama
Bhuh — Prana Svarupa
Bhuvah — Duhkha Nashaka
Svah — Sukha Svarupa
Tat — Vah (Paramatma)
Savituh — Srishti-karta Savita (Surya) Devata ka
Varenyam — Varana karane yogya, Shreshtha
Bhargah — Papa-nashaka Teja
Devasya — Divya, Deva ka
Dhimahi — Hama Dhyana Karen
Dhiyah — Buddhi ko
Yah — Jo
Nah — Hamari
Prachodayat — Shubha marga par prerit karen

|| Gayatri Shirsha ||
Om Apo Jyoti-raso-amritam Brahma Bhur-Bhuvah Svar-Om.

|| Iti Shri Gayatri Mantrah Sampurnah ||
hindiMeaning:
उस प्राणस्वरूप, दुःखनाशक, सुखस्वरूप, श्रेष्ठ, तेजस्वी, पापनाशक, देवस्वरूप परमात्मा (सविता/सूर्य) को हम ध्यान करते हैं। वह परमात्मा हमारी बुद्धि को सन्मार्ग की ओर प्रेरित करें।

गायत्री मन्त्र वेदमाता है। यह सबसे पवित्र और प्राचीन मन्त्र है जो ऋग्वेद (3.62.10) में उल्लिखित है। इस मन्त्र के ऋषि विश्वामित्र हैं, छन्द गायत्री है और देवता सविता (सूर्य) हैं। यह मन्त्र सूर्य के दिव्य तेज का ध्यान करते हुए बुद्धि की प्रेरणा की प्रार्थना है।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीगायत्रीमन्त्रस्य विश्वामित्र ऋषिः। गायत्री छन्दः। सविता देवता। ॐ भूः बीजम्। ॐ भुवः शक्तिः। ॐ स्वः कीलकम्। श्रीसवितुः प्रीत्यर्थे गायत्रीमन्त्रजपे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Supreme Vedic mantra called the Mother of all Vedas (Veda Mata)
Sharpens intellect and grants divine wisdom and discrimination
Purifies the mind, body, and soul of the chanter
Traditionally recited for removal of ignorance and leads to spiritual enlightenment
Invokes health, vitality, and protection from negative energies
Daily chanting at Sandhya time brings immense spiritual merit
description: The Gayatri Mantra, known in Sanskrit as गायत्री मन्त्र, is a powerful Vedic meditation mantra in praise of Goddess Gayatri. This sacred composition is sourced from Rigveda (3.62.10) and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to daily chanting at Sandhya time brings immense spiritual merit as well as sharpen intellect and grant divine wisdom and discrimination. Additionally, it is known to grant health, vitality, and protection from negative energies. For best results, devotees chant this on Sunday (Ravivar). The text contains 1 verses and takes around 3 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Gayatri Mantra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gayatri Mantra and where does it originate?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Mantra is the supreme 24-syllable Vedic mantra of Sanatana Dharma, originally revealed in Rigveda 3.62.10 by Sage Vishvamitra. The mantra reads: Om Bhur Bhuvah Svah, Tat Savitur Varenyam, Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi, Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat. Its viniyoga declares: Vishvamitra is the rishi, Gayatri is the chhanda (the 24-syllable Gayatri metre), Savita (the radiant Sun-deity, the source of all life and knowledge) is the devata, Om Bhuh is the bija, Om Bhuvah is the shakti, Om Svah is the kilaka. The application is for the propitiation of Sri Savita through Gayatri-mantra-japa. The mantra is venerated as Veda Mata — the Mother of all Vedas — and is the heart of the daily Sandhya Vandana of every dvija (twice-born) of Sanatana Dharma. (Sources: Rigveda Mandala 3 Sukta 62 Rik 10; Sandhya Vandana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the literal meaning of the Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: Word-by-word: Om — the pranava, the imperishable Brahman; Bhur — the earth-plane, the realm of physical existence and prana; Bhuvah — the antariksha-plane, the realm of mental space and the destruction of suffering; Svah — the celestial plane, the realm of bliss; Tat — That (the supreme Reality); Savitur — of Savita (the divine illuminator, the Sun); Varenyam — the most adorable, most worthy of being chosen; Bhargah — the radiance, the sin-destroying tejas; Devasya — of the divine; Dhimahi — may we meditate upon (let us contemplate); Dhiyah — the intellects, the buddhis; Yah — who; Nah — our; Prachodayat — may inspire, may impel forward. The complete prayer: 'Om — let us meditate upon the most adorable, sin-destroying radiance of the divine Savita, of the three worlds, who may inspire our intellects.' (Sources: Sayana's Rigveda Bhashya; classical Sandhya tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Gayatri Mantra called Veda Mata?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Mantra is venerated as Veda Mata — 'the Mother of the Vedas' — for several converging reasons. First, the mantra is the seed-shloka of the Rigveda (3.62.10), and from it (according to the Vedic tradition) all subsequent Vedic mantras are considered to be expansions. Second, the 24 syllables of the mantra correspond to the 24 cosmic principles (tattvas) and to the 24 letters that comprise the entire Sanskrit alphabet's vowel-consonant root-set. Third, the mantra is given to every dvija (twice-born brahmana, kshatriya and vaishya) at his Upanayana (sacred thread ceremony) as the brahmopadesha — the very gateway to Vedic study. Fourth, the Devi-form of Gayatri is portrayed in the Devi Bhagavata as the cosmic Mother who births, sustains and dissolves all Vedic knowledge. (Sources: Devi Bhagavata Skanda 12; Manusmriti 2.77; Gayatri upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Bhur Bhuvah Svah and the seven vyahritis?
seoFaqs.answer: Bhur Bhuvah Svah are the three principal Vyahritis (great cosmic utterances) prefixed to the core Gayatri Mantra. They name the three lokas (worlds) of the lower triad: Bhur = the Bhuloka (earth), the realm of physical existence and the prana-tattva; Bhuvah = the Bhuvarloka (atmosphere/antariksha), the realm of mental space, where bhuva also means destroyer of suffering; Svah = the Svarloka (heaven), the realm of bliss and the deva-tattva. In the extended Maha-Gayatri form, four further Vyahritis are added — Mahah (the Maharloka), Janah (the Janaloka), Tapah (the Tapoloka), and Satyam (the Satyaloka, the abode of Brahman) — completing the seven cosmic worlds (sapta-loka). The Vyahritis therefore situate Savita's radiance as illumining all seven planes of existence. (Sources: Taittiriya Aranyaka 10; Yajur Veda Mahanarayana Upanishad.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Gayatri Mantra be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gayatri Mantra is traditionally recited at the three sandhyas — pratah-sandhya (dawn, ideally during the 48-minute window before sunrise), madhyahna-sandhya (midday, when the sun is at zenith), and sayam-sandhya (dusk, the 48 minutes around sunset). The minimum prescribed sankhya is 10 japa per sandhya (Sandhya-Vandana-vidhi), with deeper practitioners doing 108, 1008 or 10,000 japa daily on a tulsi or rudraksha mala. It is especially auspicious on Sundays (Surya's day), on Gayatri Jayanti (Jyeshtha Shukla Ekadashi), on Vasant Panchami, on Surya Saptami (Ratha Saptami in Magha), on every Surya-Sankranti, during Upakarma (when the brahmana renews the Yajnopavita), on Guru Purnima, and during the Upanayana ceremony. The recitation requires bath, sacred thread properly worn, and clean asana facing east at dawn / north at noon / west at dusk. (Sources: Sandhya Vandana paddhati; Manusmriti 2.101-103.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Gayatri Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Vedic and Smarta acharyas attribute extraordinary benefits to sincere Gayatri Mantra japa: sharpening of buddhi (intellect) and the awakening of viveka (discrimination) — for the mantra explicitly prays Dhiyo yo nah prachodayat ('may He inspire our intellects'); destruction of every species of papa (sin) through Savita's bhargah (sin-destroying radiance); cultivation of sattva-guna and dispelling of tamas; the daily-renewed merit of Vedic study (svadhyaya) — Manu declares that even reciting the Gayatri 100 times daily is sufficient to redeem all sins; success in academic, scholarly and creative pursuits; perfect health through the surya-tejas of Savita; protection from negative entities and grahapida; the gradual purification of antah-karana that leads to Brahma-sakshatkara; and the supreme phala — Brahmanam Adhigachchhati — attainment of the Supreme Brahman, of which the saguna Savita is the manifest form. (Sources: Manusmriti 2.78-83; Gayatri upasana tradition.)
